texto: They call it "young music"; I see, however, that the record industry makes millions by the carload for shrewd older people! They invoke the name of spontaneity, nonconformity and originality; actually, canny "clothing industrialists" manipulate the field, undisturbed sovereigns! They call themselves revolutionaries, but the overscrupulous attentions devoted to their hair and their dress risk creating merely effeminates.
explicacao: Citação de Pope John Paul I sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “They call it "young music"; I see, however, that the record industry makes millions by the carloa…” articula reflexao, youth, youth-culture em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como youth-culture.

They call themselves revolutionaries, but the overscrupulous attentions devoted to their hair and their dress risk creating merely effeminates.

texto: Loving ourselves so much, we are naturally led to enlarge our own merits, to play down our transgressions, to judge others by different standards from those used to judge ourselves. Enlarged merits? They are described by your fellow-writer Trilussa:The little snail of VaingloryWho had crawled up an obeliskLooked at its slimy trail and said:I see I'll leave my mark on History.This is the way we are, dear Twain; even a bit of slime, if it is our own, and because it is our own, makes us boast, gives us a swelled head!
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Pope John Paul I que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “Loving ourselves so much, we are naturally led to enlarge our own merits, to play down our transg…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, humility, pride, com foco em pride.

texto: The Christian should be characterized by an effort to see things in the best light; if it is true that the word Evangelos means good news, then Christian means happy man, spreader of happiness. 'Grim faces,' Saint Philip Neri used to say, 'are not made for the merry house of Paradise!
explicacao: Orientação direta de Pope John Paul I sobre temas ligados a felicidade. A afirmação “The Christian should be characterized by an effort to see things in the best light; if it is true…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a felicidade, happiness, mood, útil em momentos de mood e optimism.

texto: When I am paid a compliment, I must compare myself with the little donkey that carried Christ on Palm Sunday. And I say to myself: If that little creature, hearing the applause of the crowd, had become proud and had begun -- jackass that he was -- to bow his thanks left and right like a prima donna, how much hilarity he would have aroused! Don't act the same!
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Pope John Paul I: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “When I am paid a compliment, I must compare myself with the little donkey that carried Christ on …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que gratidao, humility, pride pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de pride.

texto: The Republic of Venice used to boast that, in the space of three months, it could know all the events of the Mediterranean. We see the astronauts, from the distance of a few feet, at the very moment they land on the moon. Unfortunately the news almost swamps us with its frequency and abundance. It doesn't give us time to reflect: we are so constantly amazed that gradually we lose our capacity for being surprised at anything, and we don't enjoy even beautiful things.
